commands.review_summary:
The PR wraps malformed successful OpenAI ChatGPT/Codex OAuth token exchange and refresh JSON parsing in structured failures, closes callback HTML responses and accepted sockets, and adds focused regression tests.

PR surface: Source +49, Tests +52. Total +101 across 2 files.

Reproducibility: Do we have a high-confidence way to reproduce the issue? No live current-main repro was established, but the source-level path is clear: current main directly parses successful token responses with response.json() and only calls server.close() for callback cleanup.
commands.review_followup: - [P1] The remaining blocker is contributor-supplied real behavior proof for https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/issues/81865, not an automation-repairable code defect.

target.title: fix(openai): harden Codex OAuth callback cleanup
target.body:
## Summary

Fixes #81865.

This hardens the OpenAI ChatGPT/Codex OAuth flow in two places:

- token exchange and refresh now convert malformed/non-JSON successful token responses into structured OAuth failures instead of leaking a raw JSON parser exception
- the local OAuth callback server marks HTML responses as `Connection: close` and destroys accepted callback sockets during cleanup so a keep-alive callback cannot pin the auth CLI after success

This PR was prepared with AI assistance.

## Verification

- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs extensions/openai/openai-chatgpt-oauth-flow.runtime.test.ts src/plugins/provider-openai-chatgpt-oauth.test.ts`
  - Passed 2 Vitest shards: OpenAI provider OAuth facade tests and OpenAI OAuth flow tests.
- `pnpm exec oxfmt --ignore-path .gitignore extensions/openai/openai-chatgpt-oauth-flow.runtime.ts extensions/openai/openai-chatgpt-oauth-flow.runtime.test.ts`
  - Passed.
- `git diff --check HEAD~1..HEAD`
  - Passed.
- Dependency guard: checked the patch for dependency/package/lockfile changes; none changed.
- Codex review: `codex review --uncommitted`
  - No actionable regressions found in the OAuth token parsing or callback shutdown paths.

## Real behavior proof

Behavior addressed: OpenAI ChatGPT/Codex OAuth token exchange/refresh should not surface raw JSON parser exceptions for malformed successful token responses, and the localhost OAuth callback path should not leave a keep-alive callback socket able to keep the auth CLI alive after success.

Real environment tested: Local OpenClaw worktree with the OpenAI OAuth runtime tests and adjacent OpenAI provider OAuth facade tests.

Exact steps or command run after this patch: `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs extensions/openai/openai-chatgpt-oauth-flow.runtime.test.ts src/plugins/provider-openai-chatgpt-oauth.test.ts`

Evidence after fix: The focused OAuth flow tests include malformed binary-looking token exchange/refresh responses and callback response connection-closing coverage; all 13 OpenAI OAuth flow tests passed. The adjacent OpenAI provider OAuth facade shard also passed all 4 tests.

Observed result after fix: Malformed successful token responses return structured `OpenAI Codex token <exchange|refresh> response was not valid JSON` failures, and callback responses are marked connection-closing while the server cleanup destroys accepted sockets.

What was not tested: I did not perform a live browser OAuth login with real OpenAI credentials, and I did not run the full repository `pnpm build`, `pnpm check`, or full `pnpm test` suites.
target.author: abnershang
